basics.name: Rayhan Patel
basics.label: Applied AI Engineer
basics.summary: Applied AI Engineer building on-device and edge AI systems — from VLM-powered spatial reasoning on Apple Neural Engine to hybrid edge-cloud function-calling routers. Ships production AI with rigorous evaluation: 10-service backend with 5 LLM-as-a-Judge evaluators, zero SDKs. Founding engineer experience building conversational AI and healthcare ML products. MS in Applied Machine Learning at UMD.

work_authorization.post_graduation: OPT (12 months) + STEM OPT Extension (24 months) = up to 36 months post-graduation work authorization

employer_faq.does_employer_sponsor: No. CPT is NOT employer-sponsored. The university (UMD) handles all authorization. Zero cost to the employer.
employer_faq.what_employer_needs_to_do: Provide an official offer letter on company letterhead with job title, start/end dates, hours/week, and job description. That's it.
employer_faq.how_it_works: Student applies through university DSO (Designated School Official). DSO endorses Form I-20 with CPT authorization specific to that employer, location, and dates. Authorization must be approved BEFORE start date.
employer_faq.i9_verification: CPT-endorsed Form I-20 serves as valid work authorization for I-9 purposes. Standard I-9 process applies.
employer_faq.full_time_vs_part_time: Full-time CPT (>20 hrs/week) allowed during summer/breaks. Part-time CPT (≤20 hrs/week) allowed during academic terms.
employer_faq.does_cpt_affect_future_status: Part-time CPT has zero impact on OPT eligibility. Full-time CPT under 12 months also preserves full OPT eligibility.
employer_faq.ssn: F-1 students with CPT authorization are eligible for an SSN. Can apply once offer letter is received.

context.site_description: Interactive AI chatbot to learn about Rayhan's professional background, ML experience, and projects. Can answer questions about ML expertise, projects, and more!

education.program_detail: 30-credit, 10-course, non-thesis professional MS program through the Science Academy in CMNS. 6 core + 4 elective courses. Currently 2nd semester, full-time.
core_courses: MSML605: Computing Systems for Machine Learning
MSML606: Algorithms and Data Structures for ML
elective_courses_available: MSML621: Machine Learning for Wireless Communications
MSML699: Independent Study in Machine Learning

Web Analytics and Social Media Mining

key_projects_during_btech.semester_8_project: English2SQL (12-credit final year project, grade: S/10)
key_projects_during_btech.semester_7_internship: Time-Series Forecasting with LSTM (1-credit internship, grade: S/10)
key_projects_during_btech.semester_7_compiler: Compiler Construction Lab — Lex, Yacc, three-address code, optimizer (Compiler Design course + lab)
key_projects_during_btech.neurips_paper: NeurIPS 2023 MiML Workshop paper — co-authored during B.Tech (2023)

highlights: Built an e-commerce conversational agent end-to-end as a founding engineer: designed the intent router, FastAPI microservices, integrated a reranker, and implemented PII guardrails for secure data handling.
Designed a healthcare AI system to generate medical codes from electronic health records (EHR), enhancing productivity for healthcare professionals.
Implemented an LLM-as-a-judge evaluation pipeline using G-Eval to assess response quality in batch, enabling systematic hallucination measurement across the product.
Contributed to open-source frameworks Mem0 and EmbedChain by adding memory storage features for agentic workflows, improving reproducibility in multi-turn tasks.

context.product: Two products as a founding engineer: (1) E-commerce conversational agent — intent-based routing to specialized handlers for customer queries about products, orders, and support. (2) Healthcare AI system — generated medical codes from electronic health records (EHR) to enhance clinician productivity. Owned both the ML pipeline (intent classification, reranking, G-Eval, EHR coding) and the backend infrastructure (FastAPI services, GCP deployment, PII guardrails).
context.technical_challenge: Python's GIL blocking high-concurrency requests in synchronous frameworks. Cold start latency on GCP Cloud Run for LLM-serving endpoints.
context.tech_stack_selection: Chose FastAPI over Flask for native AsyncIO support, critical for I/O-bound LLM operations. Used Google Vertex AI for managed inference.
context.eval_pipeline: Implemented G-Eval (LLM-as-a-judge) for offline batch evaluation of response quality — measuring coherence, relevance, and faithfulness across production queries.
context.tenure_context: Left after 4 months to start MS in Applied Machine Learning at UMD (Aug 2025). Company is still operating. Pre-MS founding role — joined to gain industry AI/ML experience before graduate school. Clean exit on good terms.

publications.name: Building Domain-Specific LLMs Faithful To The Islamic Worldview
publications.summary: Proposed a comprehensive framework for building domain-specific LLM systems aligned with Islamic values. Evaluated prompt engineering (zero-shot, few-shot, instruction-based), RAG over 54k Hadith dataset, and fine-tuning GPT-3.5 on IslamQA. Fine-tuning on Hadiths (10k) + IslamQA (400) achieved best F1 of 0.402 with 0.078 embedding distance.
publications.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable performance across numerous natural language understanding use cases. However, this impressive performance comes with inherent limitations, such as the tendency to perpetuate stereotypical biases or fabricate non-existent facts. In the context of Islam and its representation, accurate and factual representation of its beliefs and teachings rooted in the Quran and Sunnah is key. This work focuses on the challenge of building domain-specific LLMs faithful to the Islamic worldview and proposes ways to build and evaluate such systems. Firstly, we define this open-ended goal as a technical problem and propose various solutions. Subsequently, we critically examine known challenges inherent to each approach and highlight evaluation methodologies that can be used to assess such systems.

context.research_premise: Existing LLMs exhibit Western bias and hallucinate on domain-specific religious content. Investigated whether prompt engineering, RAG, and fine-tuning could align models with the Islamic worldview.
context.methodology_detail: Evaluated GPT-3.5, GPT-4, Llama-7B, and Llama-70B across zero-shot, few-shot, and instruction-based prompting. Built RAG system over 54k Hadith dataset using OpenAI ada-002 embeddings. Fine-tuned GPT-3.5 turbo on Hadiths (10k) and IslamQA (400 questions).
context.eval_strategy: Used BERTScore (precision, recall, F1) and embedding distance (cosine similarity via ada-002) on 100 randomly selected IslamQA questions, comparing model responses to scholar-generated answers.
context.key_results: Few-shot prompting consistently best across all models. Fine-tuning GPT-3.5 on Hadiths + IslamQA achieved F1=0.402, best overall. RAG alone did not show significant improvement over prompting. Paper also implemented guardrails using RAIL (Reliable AI Markup Language).
context.my_contribution: Developed the evaluation frameworks to measure alignment and accuracy (BERTScore + embedding distance metrics). Presented the research at the NeurIPS 2023 MiML Workshop.
context.author_position: 3rd of 3 authors. Research conducted during B.Tech (2023), published December 2023.

projects.name: PathGuard Edge
projects.description: On-device spatial safety intelligence for construction workers — dual-pipeline edge AI system fusing VLM scene understanding with real-time spatial hazard detection
highlights: Architected a dual-pipeline edge AI system: a Cactus Narrator running Liquid LFM 2.5 VLM (1.6B params) on-device for scene transcription, feeding dynamic prompts into a spatial reasoning HUD.

Built a real-time hazard detection pipeline fusing Grounding DINO (zero-shot detection), SAM2 (pixel-precise segmentation), and Depth Anything V2 (distance estimation) inside an egocentric corridor ROI.
Engineered a corridor occupancy metric and state machine (Clear → Obstacle Ahead → Trip Risk) with audio alerts, outputting enriched telemetry JSON with physical risk events for site managers.

context.problem_space: 800+ construction workers die annually from falls/trips. Cloud-based solutions fail because job sites lack reliable connectivity. Spatial safety intelligence must live on the edge.
context.architecture_decision: Dual pipeline: VLM generates scene-specific prompts (avoiding static/hallucinated detection lists), then spatial pipeline performs corridor-bounded hazard reasoning. Classical CV fallback runs every frame; DINO runs on a schedule.
context.key_innovation: Corridor ROI — a trapezoid representing the worker's egocentric walking lane. All hazard reasoning is bounded to this region, eliminating false positives from objects outside the path.
context.integration_detail: Enriched telemetry JSON bridges Cactus semantic report with PathGuard physical data: peak corridor occupancy, depth buckets (near/mid/far), and trip risk event timestamps.
context.hackathon_context: UMD × Ironsite Startup Shell Hackathon (Feb 2026). $17,500 prize pool ($10k/$5k/$2.5k). ~60 participants. Focused on spatial/physical intelligence and world models. Submissions required GitHub repo, detailed README, and 3-5 min demo video. Applied Cactus SDK knowledge gained from FunctionGemma hackathon the same weekend.
context.my_contribution: Built the Cactus Narrator integration (on-device VLM via Liquid LFM 2.5 on Apple Neural Engine), wrote the hybrid routing logic (confidence-based on-device/cloud escalation with cooldown timer), created the dynamic prompt generation pipeline (VLM scene descriptions → Gemini extraction → GroundedDINO-compatible prompt list), and merged both sub-projects into one unified codebase under extreme time pressure. Also assisted Suraj Rao and Keshav Naram on the PathGuard HUD side (corridor reasoning, state machine). Recorded and narrated the demo video.
context.team: Rayhan Patel (Lead — Cactus integration, project merge, demo), Suraj Rao (PathGuard HUD core — detection, depth, corridor), Keshav Naram (PathGuard HUD — fallback CV, state machine, Streamlit UI)

projects.name: FunctionGemma Hybrid Router
projects.description: 3-tier hybrid AI router orchestrating FunctionGemma-270M on-device and Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite in the cloud for optimized function-calling
highlights: Built a 3-tier hybrid routing system orchestrating FunctionGemma-270M on-device (via Cactus SDK) with Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite cloud fallback for function-calling tasks.
Achieved 99% function-calling accuracy at 548ms average latency by designing intelligent edge/cloud routing logic that prioritizes local inference.
Developed at the Cactus × Google DeepMind Global Hackathon (Feb 2026); applied learnings directly to build PathGuard Edge.

context.hackathon_rubrics: Judged on: (1) quality of hybrid routing algorithm, (2) end-to-end products executing function calls, (3) low-latency voice-to-action.
context.architecture_decision: 3 tiers: Tier 1 = FunctionGemma-270M on-device for fast/private execution. Tier 2 = confidence-based routing with semantic validation gate. Tier 3 = Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite cloud fallback for complex tasks. Pre-routing intelligence scores prompt difficulty 0.0-1.0 before inference.
context.engineering_details: Engineered 5-step pipeline: (1) Pre-Routing Intelligence via lexical difficulty scoring, (2) Semantic Validation Gate catching tool-name hallucinations, (3) Deterministic NLP argument extraction fixing the 270M model's broken JSON integer parsing, (4) Multi-Intent Cloud Merging that preserves valid local calls while filling gaps via cloud, (5) Aggressive latency slicing with Singleton genai client cache saving ~100ms TLS handshake per call.
context.metrics_detail: 0.99 F1 accuracy score on 30-case benchmark (10 easy / 10 medium / 10 hard). 70% on-device ratio. 548ms average latency. Composite score ~80.9% (formula: 0.60*F1 + 0.15*time_score + 0.25*on_device_ratio). Baseline started at ~50% F1, iterated through 62.3% → 65.3% → final 0.99 F1 by fixing confidence_threshold, cloud_handoff handling, and argument post-processing.
context.my_contribution: Solo-engineered the entire system. Designed and implemented the 3-Tier Pre-Routing algorithm, integrated FunctionGemma-270M on-device via Cactus SDK, built the Semantic Validation Gate, deterministic NLP argument parser, Multi-Intent Cloud Merging, and latency optimizations. Debugged model weight corruption, confidence_threshold tuning, and Gemini API rate limiting.
context.outcome: 2nd place. Won $5,000 in Gemini API credits. Achieved near-perfect 0.99 F1. Applied Cactus SDK and edge AI knowledge directly to PathGuard Edge the same weekend.
context.event: Cactus × Google DeepMind Global Hackathon (Feb 21, 2026), hosted by AI Tinkerers DC at University of Maryland. Six-city global event.

projects.name: AI Resume Chatbot
projects.description: Production AI agent answering recruiter questions — 10 backend services, 5 LLM-as-a-Judge evaluators, zero SDKs. Live at chat.rayhanpatel.com
highlights: Built a service-oriented AI agent with 10 backend services (Intent Classifier, Agent, Memory, Tracer, Rate Limiter, Session, Lead Capture, Job Extractor, Job Parser, Prompt Generator) and graceful degradation — chat continues even if every external service is down.

Replaced the blocking Google GenAI SDK with direct httpx HTTP/2 calls to the Gemini REST API, implementing async connection pooling (20 keepalive, 100 max connections), a thread-safe TTLCache with deadlock prevention, and pure ASGI middleware to avoid Starlette's BaseHTTPMiddleware hanging bug.

Deployed 5 automated LLM-as-a-Judge evaluators (Hallucination, Relevance, Conciseness, Helpfulness, Toxicity) via Langfuse at 100% sampling with zero latency impact — evaluation model (Gemini 2.5 Flash) is separate from generation model to avoid self-bias.

context.architecture: Split Architecture: Frontend on Vercel (through Cloudflare DNS+SSL), Backend on Railway (direct API access bypassing Cloudflare to avoid SSE timeout limits). Service-Oriented Architecture — thin FastAPI route handlers delegate to rich domain services. Every external dependency (Mem0, Supabase, Langfuse) wrapped in a service class with 'enabled' flag that fails gracefully.
context.httpx_decision: The official google-genai Python SDK uses synchronous transport internally, causing blocking I/O and threading issues with asyncio. Replaced with direct httpx HTTP/2 calls to Gemini REST API (httpx >=0.28). Provides full async support, connection pooling (20 keepalive, 100 max), and explicit control over timeouts and retries.
context.ttlcache_decision: TTLCache extends OrderedDict with threading.Lock on all public methods. Internal operations use super().__delitem__() and super().__setitem__() to bypass overridden methods that would re-acquire the lock, preventing deadlocks.
context.security_layers: 8-layer SSRF-protected job URL fetcher, Cloudflare Turnstile bot detection (Managed Mode), HTML stripping via @field_validator(mode='before') on all Pydantic models, secrets.compare_digest() for constant-time admin auth, prompt injection defense with <<<JOB_DESCRIPTION_START>>> markers.
context.intent_classification: Off-topic queries classified and declined BEFORE the expensive agent call. Uses Gemini Flash (cheapest model) with temperature=0.1 for deterministic output. Defaults to job_related on any failure.
context.eval_pipeline: 5 LLM-as-a-Judge evaluators run server-side in Langfuse at 100% sampling: Hallucination, Relevance, Conciseness, Helpfulness, Toxicity. Evaluation model (Gemini 2.5 Flash) differs from generation model (Gemini 2.0 Flash) to avoid self-bias. Zero latency impact on users.
context.key_features_summary: 25+ features: real-time SSE streaming, dual LLM fallback (Vertex AI → AI Studio), job URL extraction + LLM-powered parsing, dynamic starter prompts, semantic memory (Mem0), session persistence (Supabase + TTLCache fallback), dark/light theme, share conversation, keyboard shortcuts, lead capture (Google Sheets), WCAG accessibility, React ErrorBoundary, OG+Twitter Card SEO, iOS mobile polish.
context.tech_stack_detail: Backend: FastAPI >=0.115, uvicorn >=0.34, httpx >=0.28, Pydantic >=2.10, mem0ai >=1.0.2, langfuse >=3.12, supabase >=2.27, tenacity >=8.2, google-auth >=2.27. Frontend: React 18.3, Vite 5.4, TailwindCSS 3.4, lucide-react, react-markdown 10.1, remark-gfm 4.0.

projects.name: AlphaFoundry (Quant Engine)
projects.description: Factor-based quantitative investment strategy engine using XGBoost and Fama-French factors
highlights: Architected a rolling-window backtesting engine that combines Fama-French 5-factor analysis with XGBoost Learning-to-Rank, achieving a Sharpe Ratio of 0.95 (vs SPY 0.85).
Built a production-grade FastAPI inference service for real-time asset ranking.
Implemented Walk-Forward validation to prevent look-ahead bias in financial time-series modeling.

context.problem_space: Retail investors lack access to systematic, factor-based strategies that hedge funds use. Most open-source quant tools focus on single-factor or momentum only.
context.architecture_decision: Monthly rebalancing on Walk-Forward validation to prevent data leakage. Research environment (Jupyter) + production FastAPI inference API (/topk, /health) that ranks 500+ S&P assets on-the-fly using live factor data.
context.strategy_overview: Data Ingestion (S&P 500 daily + Fama-French factors) → Feature Engineering (Rolling Betas: Market, SMB, HML, RMW, CMA) → Alpha Generation (OLS Rolling Regression as base, XGBoost Ranker on 36-month lookback as ML model) → Portfolio Selection (Top-K decile equal-weighted).
context.performance_benchmark: 2016-2025 Out-of-Sample Backtest: AlphaFoundry XGB achieved 14.2% annualized return (vs SPY 11.8%), 0.95 Sharpe Ratio (vs SPY 0.85), -18.4% max drawdown (vs SPY -24.5%). Note: backtested data, does not guarantee future results.

projects.name: English2SQL
projects.description: Natural Language to SQL converter using RAG and LLMs — B.Tech final year project (Semester 8)
highlights: Implemented NL-to-SQL over relational/star schemas; applied RAG + prompt optimization on a 120-query eval set for a measurable accuracy lift.
Achieved +9% execution accuracy lift on evaluation suite; built clean API for offline/online testing.
Exposed REST endpoints with FastAPI and Docker; added unit tests and regression scripts for reliable, end-to-end evaluation.

context.problem_space: Business users struggle to write complex SQL queries. Existing text-to-SQL models fail on schema-specific column names.
context.solution_detail: Injected the database schema (DDL) into the RAG context window. Used few-shot prompting with 5 examples of similar queries to guide the LLM.
context.tech_detail: Production-style NL→SQL app: FastAPI + LangChain SQL chain over Postgres (AWS RDS) with pluggable LLMs (OpenAI base/fine-tuned, Ollama). Generates & executes SQL, returns JSON. Schema-aware context/RAG, Pydantic, CORS, logging/LangSmith tracing.
context.metric_explanation: Used Exact Match (SQL syntax matches ground truth) vs Execution Accuracy (Result matches ground truth). The +9% lift was in Execution Accuracy.
context.my_contribution: Team project (B.Tech final year, 12 credits). Rayhan contributed to the backend and RAG pipeline.

projects.name: Compiler Construction Lab
projects.description: Suite of compiler construction modules in C and Python — lexical analysis (Lex), syntax analysis (Yacc), three-address code generation, and code optimization. B.Tech Semester 7 course project.
highlights: Built lexical analysis modules using Flex (Lex) for tokenizing C source code — extracting keywords, identifiers, constants, and operators.
Implemented syntax analysis using Bison (Yacc) for grammar validation of variable declarations and control structures, plus an arithmetic expression evaluator with precedence rules.
Developed three-address code generator converting expressions to intermediate representation (quadruples), and a code optimizer implementing constant folding and dead code elimination.

projects.name: Time-Series Forecasting
projects.description: LSTM-based stock price prediction pipeline — B.Tech Semester 7 internship project
highlights: Built an end-to-end time-series workflow (scaling, 60-day sequence windows, multi-layer LSTM) using TensorFlow/Keras for stock price forecasting.
Tracked MAE/RMSE and compared against a naive last-value baseline on holdout data.

projects.name: Supervised Classification Pipeline (Titanic)
projects.description: Random Forest classifier with basic feature engineering — B.Tech Semester 7 internship project
highlights: Built a Random Forest classifier with feature engineering (passenger class, sex, age, fare) for Titanic survival prediction.
Applied standard preprocessing (missing values, encoding, scaling) and evaluated on test set.

projects.name: Personal Infrastructure (Home Lab)
projects.description: Self-hosted AI and media infrastructure across Raspberry Pi 4 and custom PC (RTX 2080 Super)
highlights: Built a self-hosted media and AI lab: Plex media server and VPN on Raspberry Pi 4 with Nginx reverse proxy and static IP.
Hosted local LLMs via Ollama on a custom PC (RTX 2080 Super, AMD 3800X, 32GB RAM) for private AI inference, accessible across devices.
Currently experimenting with Crawl4AI web crawling framework for automated data collection pipelines.

context.hardware: Two-node setup: (1) Raspberry Pi 4 — Plex media server, VPN, Nginx reverse proxy with static IP. (2) Custom PC — RTX 2080 Super GPU, AMD Ryzen 7 3800X, 32GB RAM — runs Ollama for local LLM inference (accessed remotely for personal private chat). Currently exploring Crawl4AI deployment.
context.motivation: Hands-on experience with self-hosting, GPU-accelerated inference, and network administration. Demonstrates comfort with Linux, hardware, and DevOps outside of coursework.

volunteer.summary: 2nd place ($5,000 Gemini API credits). Solo-built FunctionGemma hybrid router (0.99 F1, 548ms, 70% on-device). Six-city global event. Applied learnings to PathGuard Edge.

Built PathGuard Edge: on-device spatial safety intelligence for construction workers. $17.5k prize pool, ~60 participants. Dual-pipeline edge AI fusing VLM scene understanding with real-time hazard detection.
Developed an advanced e-commerce AI agent system. Achieved finalist recognition among participants at this VC-hosted hackathon.
Led team strategies and coordinated practice schedules.
